lines.text: I'm on the East Side near Schenk's Corners. The clock reads {{CLOCK}}, we're {{ELAPSED}} into the night. Madison-wide kill count is holding at 38, which is actually down from 42 at this point last year — we're running 9.5% below YoY pace.

Power grid failure just took out three blocks around Atwood Avenue. I'm running on a flashlight and my phone is at 12%. The backup generator for the substation is down — operator's voice came through on the emergency band, said they can't access it, which means nobody can. People are barricading by candlelight now.
East Side is still the hottest zone — 34% of the night's kills so far. But the blackout is actually suppressing activity in that corridor. Hard to hunt when you can't see.

Hold on, my laptop just lost the network feed. I'm switching to the secondary — no, that's also down. Tim, are you reading this? I'm going to reposition north toward the Beltline where I still have line of sight.
I'm moving toward the overpass now. Visibility is maybe 40 feet in this dark. I can hear a vehicle engine somewhere past Northside Drive and voices — I'm going to get closer to the —

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide: 38 kills at {{CLOCK}} (9.5% below YoY pace)
POWER OUTAGE: Three blocks East Side/Atwood. Substation backup offline. Backup generator operator unable to access facility.
East Side dominance: 34% of night's kills. Blackout suppressing activity in corridor.
Tucker field unit repositioning. Signal degradation in blackout zone.
